preview_text:
The model rates Netherlands as the clear favorite, assigning them a 70% win probability against Tunisia's 8.5%. This 61-percentage-point gap reflects a substantial disparity in the forecast, with the model viewing the Netherlands as the likely victors on a neutral ground.

The expected distribution tilts sharply toward a Netherlands victory, but the 22% draw probability indicates meaningful competitive space. A stalemate remains more likely than a Tunisia win, suggesting the match could remain close tactically even if Netherlands hold the upper hand in overall quality. The outcome is not a foregone conclusion, though one-sided results lean heavily toward the favorite.

Model and market estimates are broadly aligned. Sportsbooks place Netherlands at 72% win (2.5 points above the model) and draw at 18% (4 points below), while Polymarket implies an even wider Netherlands edge at 74%. These small divergences sit within typical consensus variation and suggest no material disagreement between the statistical forecast and betting expectations on this fixture.
